seed_expert_discoveries.project: Custom LLM Router APIs + Fable + GPT-5.6 Sol
seed_expert_discoveries.technical_takeaway: Bindu Reddy shares progress on custom router APIs (95%+ accuracy) for mixing LLMs, enabling users to build agents combining Fable's one-shot coding with GPT-5.6 Sol for cheap/fast inference on complex tasks.
Harrison Chase announces Deep Agents as an open-source, model-agnostic agent harness with 4 core capabilities; new LangChain Academy course covers harnesses, tracing, and deployment via LangSmith.
Harrison Chase polls on standardizing ATIF (or alternatives) for agent traces in production runtimes, highlighting the current 'wild west' state of agent observability.
Vaibhav Srivastav highlights OpenAI's upcoming GPT-5.6 Sol (with Terra/Luna) launch for Codex/Chat, emphasizing agentic workflows and real-time prompting loops.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Decentralized AI validators for agent contract disputes using optimistic democracy and intelligent contracts to handle subjective real-world logic beyond traditional smart contracts.
near_misses.reason: Mentions agentic economy and $9T spend but lacks specific technical implementation details or benchmarks from the past 24h.

near_misses.technical_takeaway: Updated evaluations with Fable 5, Sonnet 5; new First-Try Rate metric for model differentiation in agent tasks.
near_misses.reason: Agent eval framework update but primarily marketing/OSS release note without deep technical innovation or benchmarks.

notes: Seed experts yielded several high-signal agent and LLM tooling updates; global keyword search returned mostly unrelated or low-signal posts, so relevant agent mentions moved to near_misses. No posts found from most seed experts in the window.
